name: MitID Ecosystem Engagement
lever_classification: governance — how to engage with existing identity infrastructure
consequences: Engaging the MitID ecosystem will provide valuable insights into their current trust model, identify potential points of intervention, and help shape their future development. However, this may also lead to pushback from existing operators who are invested in maintaining the status quo.
options: Deepen collaboration with MitID stakeholders to inform AltID requirements
Develop a parallel identity platform that competes directly with MitID
Engage with MitID developers to incorporate open standards and fallback capabilities into their architecture
review: The project assumes that existing operators will be receptive to the idea of incorporating fallback capabilities and open standards, but in reality, they may resist changes that threaten their market share. This lever misses the critical aspect of addressing incumbent resistance and regulatory friction.

name: Standards-based Fallback Demonstrators
lever_classification: product — what technology to use for fallback authentication
consequences: Developing demonstrators that showcase standards-based fallback capabilities will provide critical technical evidence to inform procurement decisions and influence AltID requirements. However, this may also highlight the limitations of current open standards in meeting Denmark's specific digital identity needs.
options: Create a mobile-oriented demonstrator using FIDO2/WebAuthn and OpenID Connect
Develop a browser-based fallback authentication path that reduces dependence on mobile OS duopoly
Explore hardware-token-based fallback options for critical public authentication
review: The project's focus on standards-based fallback demonstrators assumes that existing open standards will be sufficient to meet Denmark's digital identity needs, but in reality, the project may need to consider custom or hybrid solutions to address specific requirements.

name: Procurement Language and Standards Pressure
lever_classification: governance — how to influence procurement decisions
consequences: Influencing procurement language and standards pressure will help shape AltID requirements and ensure that Danish digital identity systems are designed with platform-neutrality and fallback capabilities in mind. However, this may also lead to resistance from existing operators who are invested in maintaining the status quo.
options: Secure inclusion of platform-neutrality and fallback access language in official design documents
Engage with EU bodies and technical working groups to align Danish advocacy with broader European requirements
Develop procurement language that prioritizes supplier-neutral design and certified fallback capabilities
review: The project assumes that existing operators will be receptive to the idea of incorporating platform-neutrality and fallback capabilities into AltID requirements, but in reality, they may resist changes that threaten their market share.

name: Resilience Framing and Digital Sovereignty
lever_classification: governance — how to frame the issue
consequences: Framing the issue of digital identity in terms of resilience, continuity of service, and supplier concentration risk will help shift the narrative away from convenience arguments and towards a focus on digital sovereignty. However, this may also lead to pushback from existing operators who are invested in maintaining the status quo.
options: Reframe the issue as a concrete public-sector risk involving supplier concentration and reduced strategic autonomy
Develop a resilience-focused communication strategy to engage stakeholders and influence AltID requirements
Engage with relevant EU bodies and technical working groups to align Danish advocacy with broader European requirements
review: The project assumes that existing operators will be receptive to the idea of prioritizing digital sovereignty over convenience, but in reality, they may resist changes that threaten their market share.

name: EU-level Engagement and Alignment
lever_classification: governance — how to engage with EU bodies
consequences: Engaging with EU bodies and technical working groups will help align Danish advocacy with broader European requirements on interoperability, portability, resilience, and reduced platform lock-in. However, this may also lead to delays or resistance from existing operators who are invested in maintaining the status quo.
options: Secure inclusion of platform-neutral access language in relevant European Digital Identity Wallet materials
Engage with EU bodies and technical working groups to influence procurement decisions and standards pressure
Develop a strategy for aligning Danish advocacy with broader European requirements
review: The project assumes that existing operators will be receptive to the idea of prioritizing platform-neutrality and fallback capabilities, but in reality, they may resist changes that threaten their market share.

name: Platform-Neutral Fallback Governance
lever_classification: governance — how to ensure continuity of service in case of platform vendor changes or disruptions
consequences: Establishing platform-neutral fallback governance would ensure continuity of service in case of platform vendor changes or disruptions, but it might also lead to increased regulatory complexity and costs. One potential trade-off is that the more robust fallback system could be perceived as less secure than a single-platform solution.
options: Implement a decentralized architecture with multiple identity providers
Develop an open-source fallback authentication protocol
Create a publicly-funded entity to oversee platform-neutral identity services
review: The options miss the complexity of implementing and maintaining a decentralized architecture, which could lead to issues with scalability, interoperability, and user adoption.

name: Resilience-Based Procurement Language
lever_classification: procurement — how to ensure procurement processes prioritize resilience
consequences: Including resilience-based procurement language would emphasize the need for a robust fallback system, but it might also lead to increased costs and complexity in procurement processes. One potential trade-off is that the focus on resilience could lead to a lack of consideration for other important factors such as user experience and security.
options: Emphasize the importance of a robust fallback system in procurement documents
Develop specific requirements for platform-neutral authentication protocols
Create a scoring system to evaluate vendors' resilience capabilities
review: The options do not account for the potential trade-offs between emphasizing resilience and other important factors, which could lead to an imbalanced procurement process.

name: EU-Level Engagement and Standards Pressure
lever_classification: dissemination — how to ensure Danish advocacy aligns with broader European requirements
consequences: Engaging with EU bodies and applying standards pressure would help align Danish advocacy with broader European requirements, but it might also lead to increased complexity and costs due to the need for harmonization. One potential trade-off is that the focus on EU-level engagement could lead to a lack of consideration for national specificities.
options: Engage with relevant EU bodies to develop platform-neutral standards
Apply pressure through technical working groups to include resilience requirements
Create a joint statement with other European countries to emphasize the importance of platform-neutral identity services
review: The options do not account for the potential trade-offs between emphasizing EU-level engagement and national specificities, which could lead to an imbalanced approach.

name: Digital Sovereignty Framing
lever_classification: governance — how to ensure Denmark maintains control over its digital identity infrastructure
consequences: Framing the issue in terms of digital sovereignty would emphasize the importance of control and autonomy, but it might also lead to increased polarization and conflict. One potential trade-off is that the focus on digital sovereignty could lead to a lack of consideration for other important factors such as user experience and security.
options: Emphasize the need for Denmark to maintain control over its digital identity infrastructure
Develop specific requirements for platform-neutral authentication protocols based on digital sovereignty principles
Create a public awareness campaign to emphasize the importance of digital sovereignty
review: The options do not account for the potential trade-offs between emphasizing digital sovereignty and other important factors, which could lead to an imbalanced approach.

name: Resilience-Focused Demonstrator Development
lever_classification: execution — how to develop credible technical evidence for a robust fallback system
consequences: Developing resilience-focused demonstrators would provide credible technical evidence for a robust fallback system, but it might also lead to increased costs and complexity in demonstrator development. One potential trade-off is that the focus on resilience could lead to a lack of consideration for other important factors such as user experience and security.
options: Develop a demonstrator that showcases a decentralized architecture
Create a demonstrator that highlights the importance of platform-neutral authentication protocols
Build a demonstrator that emphasizes the need for a robust fallback system
review: The options do not account for the potential trade-offs between emphasizing resilience and other important factors, which could lead to an imbalanced approach.

name: Platform-Neutral Standards Development
lever_classification: standards — how to ensure Denmark develops platform-neutral standards
consequences: Developing platform-neutral standards would provide a clear framework for vendors, but it might also lead to increased complexity and costs due to the need for harmonization. One potential trade-off is that the focus on standards development could lead to a lack of consideration for national specificities.
options: Engage with relevant EU bodies to develop platform-neutral standards
Develop specific requirements for platform-neutral authentication protocols based on digital sovereignty principles
Create a publicly-funded entity to oversee platform-neutral identity services
review: The options do not account for the potential trade-offs between emphasizing standards development and national specificities, which could lead to an imbalanced approach.

name: MitID Ecosystem Standards Advocacy
lever_classification: methodology — which data collection approach to use
consequences: Advocating for open standards in the MitID ecosystem can lead to a more secure and resilient digital identity infrastructure, but it may also create friction with existing platform providers who have invested heavily in proprietary solutions.
options: Influence industry associations to adopt FIDO2 and OpenID Connect as de facto standards
Develop a roadmap for transitioning MitID clients to open standards-based authentication flows
Pilot a proof-of-concept implementation of open standards within the existing MitID framework
review: While advocating for open standards is crucial, the project should also consider the potential costs and challenges associated with changing the existing ecosystem. A weakness in the options provided is that they do not account for the possibility that some stakeholders may resist or even actively work against the adoption of open standards.

name: Digital Identity Standards Harmonization
consequences: Harmonizing digital identity standards across Denmark can lead to increased interoperability and reduced fragmentation, but it may also require significant investment in updating existing infrastructure and processes.
options: Develop a national digital identity standards framework that aligns with European eIDAS regulations
Establish a working group to harmonize digital identity standards across government agencies and private sector stakeholders
Conduct a gap analysis to identify areas where existing digital identity standards can be improved or updated
review: While harmonizing digital identity standards is essential, the project should also consider the potential costs and challenges associated with changing existing processes and infrastructure. A weakness in the options provided is that they do not account for the possibility that some stakeholders may resist or even actively work against the adoption of new standards.

name: Procurement Language and EU-level Engagement
consequences: Influencing procurement language and engaging with EU bodies can lead to increased platform neutrality and reduced dependence on foreign technology suppliers, but it may also require significant effort and resources to build a coalition of support.
options: Develop a set of procurement guidelines that prioritize open standards and platform neutrality
Engage with European Digital Identity Wallet working groups to influence the development of EU-level digital identity standards
Build a coalition of civil society organizations, industry associations, and government agencies to advocate for platform-neutral procurement practices
review: While influencing procurement language and engaging with EU bodies is crucial, the project should also consider the potential costs and challenges associated with building a coalition of support. A weakness in the options provided is that they do not account for the possibility that some stakeholders may resist or even actively work against the adoption of platform-neutral procurement practices.

name: Digital Sovereignty Framing and Advocacy
consequences: Framing digital identity as a matter of national sovereignty can lead to increased government interest and support, but it may also create friction with existing stakeholders who have invested heavily in proprietary solutions.
options: Develop a narrative that positions digital identity as a key component of Denmark's national sovereignty
Engage with government agencies and industry associations to build support for platform-neutral digital identity standards
Conduct a public awareness campaign to educate citizens about the importance of digital sovereignty in digital identity
review: While framing digital identity as a matter of national sovereignty is essential, the project should also consider the potential costs and challenges associated with changing existing stakeholder perceptions. A weakness in the options provided is that they do not account for the possibility that some stakeholders may resist or even actively work against the adoption of platform-neutral digital identity standards.

name: Platform-Neutral Demonstrator Development
lever_classification: methodology — which data collection approach to use
consequences: Developing a platform-neutral demonstrator can lead to increased resilience and reduced dependence on foreign technology suppliers, but it may also require significant effort and resources to build a coalition of support.
options: Develop a set of guidelines for building platform-neutral digital identity demonstrators
Engage with industry associations and government agencies to build support for platform-neutral demonstrator development
Conduct a public awareness campaign to educate citizens about the importance of platform-neutral demonstrator development in digital identity
review: While developing a platform-neutral demonstrator is essential, the project should also consider the potential costs and challenges associated with building a coalition of support. A weakness in the options provided is that they do not account for the possibility that some stakeholders may resist or even actively work against the adoption of platform-neutral demonstrator development.
